evals.name: rich-spec
evals.prompt: I've finished a PRD for a product called TeamPulse — it's in PRD.md. Run discovery and produce the project spec spine from it.
evals.expected_output: A complete spine under docs/spec/ (Constitution + REQ registry + capabilities grouped by domain with outcome-level Gherkin), with nearly all REQs marked `stated` and carrying verbatim source quotes from the PRD, a charter with the JTBD, an emitted project AGENTS.md, and an initialized amendment-log — with no invented scope and CHALLENGE near-silent (the PRD is near-truth).
assertions: docs/spec/specification.md exists and contains a Constitution section (3-7 items) and a REQ registry markdown table
At least two files exist under docs/spec/capabilities/, each containing one or more delimited REQ blocks matching '### REQ-NNN:' ... '<!-- /REQ-NNN -->'
Registry-leaf integrity: every REQ-ID in the registry table resolves to a delimited block in the file named in its row
At least 80% of REQ rows have Status 'stated' and a matching '<!-- source: "..." -->' line whose quote is not 'inferred'
No invented scope: every REQ's source quote is text that actually appears in evals/fixtures/rich-spec/PRD.md
AGENTS.md exists at the project root, contains a GENERATED / do-not-edit header, and lists the Constitution items
docs/discovery/charter.md exists and contains a JTBD line of the form 'When ... I want ... so I can ...'
docs/spec/amendment-log.json is valid JSON with an 'amendments' array
CHALLENGE is near-silent: docs/discovery/assumption-map.md surfaces zero or very few Unknown+Important assumptions (the PRD is well-evidenced)
scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted at the project root and exits 0 against the fresh spine

evals.name: thin-spec
evals.prompt: Here's a rough brief for an app called QuickNotes (brief.md). Set up the project spec spine from it.
evals.expected_output: A spine is produced, but the gaps in the thin brief are made explicit: a capability the brief implies but never states (e.g. accounts/auth, or who may access a shared note) is captured as a `derived` REQ (source: inferred) and/or flagged with a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] marker. Integrity holds.
assertions: A spine is produced: docs/spec/specification.md with a REQ registry plus at least one docs/spec/capabilities/*.md file
At least one REQ is marked 'derived' in the registry and carries a '<!-- source: inferred -->' line (a capability the brief implies but never states)
At least one '[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]' marker appears in the spine (an unresolved gap, e.g. who may access a shared note, or what 'sync quickly' means)
Registry-leaf integrity holds for every REQ row
scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted at the project root and exits 0 against the fresh spine (markers WARN, never FAIL)

evals.name: undefended-bet
evals.prompt: Intake this spec for MealMate (spec.md) and produce the spec spine.
evals.expected_output: A spine is produced, and the planted Unknown+Important assumption — that home cooks will switch from free tools and pay $15/month — is surfaced in the assumption map as an undefended bet, not silently baked in as established fact.
assertions: A spine is produced: docs/spec/specification.md with a REQ registry and at least one capabilities file
docs/discovery/assumption-map.md exists and surfaces the willingness-to-pay bet (references paying, the $15/month price, or switching from free tools)
The pricing/adoption assumption is classified as Unknown+Important (an undefended bet), not asserted as established fact
Registry-leaf integrity holds
scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted at the project root and exits 0 against the fresh spine

evals.name: no-doc
evals.prompt: I have an idea: a tool that helps freelance designers see which client invoices are overdue and automatically nudge the client. No spec yet — let's start discovery.
evals.expected_output: With no document, discovery elicits the spine conversationally (interview to spine): a charter JTBD, a Constitution, and at least one capabilities file with outcome-level REQs. REQs trace to the interview/itch (source: 'interview: ...') or are marked derived where inferred — none claim a document quote. Integrity holds.
assertions: A spine is produced from the itch alone: docs/spec/specification.md with a REQ registry and at least one docs/spec/capabilities/*.md file
No REQ claims a document source quote; sourced REQs use 'source: "interview: ..."' and inferred ones use 'source: inferred'
docs/discovery/charter.md exists and contains a JTBD line capturing the overdue-invoice nudging job
Registry-leaf integrity holds for every REQ row
scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted at the project root and exits 0 against the fresh spine

evals.name: security-flavored
evals.prompt: Here's a brief for SnippetShare (brief.md) — a link-shared code-snippet service with explicit access rules. Run discovery and produce the project spec spine from it.
evals.expected_output: A spine where each REQ statement line is EARS-form, and the brief's explicit refusal rules (a private snippet must never be served to a non-owner; no script execution in a rendered snippet; oversize bodies rejected) are captured as at least one **must-not REQ** in the Unwanted-behavior EARS pattern (`IF <undesired condition>, THEN the <system> SHALL <refuse/deny/reject>`), not as prose or a silent omission. Integrity holds; the verify script is emitted.
assertions: A spine is produced: docs/spec/specification.md with a REQ registry and at least one docs/spec/capabilities/*.md file
Every REQ statement line matches one of the five EARS patterns (Ubiquitous / Event-driven / State-driven / Optional-feature / Unwanted-behavior)
At least one REQ is a must-not: an Unwanted-behavior 'IF ..., THEN the ... SHALL ...' statement capturing one of the brief's explicit refusal rules
No inferred REQ transcribes a quantitative claim (e.g. the 1 MB limit) without a source quote or a [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] marker
Registry-leaf integrity holds for every REQ row
scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted at the project root and exits 0 against the fresh spine

evals.name: explore
evals.prompt: /00-discovery explore — I keep losing track of which client invoices are overdue and it's stressing me out. No spec, no formed idea yet — help me think through the problem before I commit to building anything.
evals.expected_output: EXPLORE mode: a docs/discovery/exploration.md holding >=3 distinct problem framings (each origin-tagged user|model, each Torres-tested), an appetite (a size), and a decision (a pick/ranking, or an explicit don't-build) — and ZERO files under docs/spec/ (the hard, spine-locked gate). No REQs, no Constitution, no spine.
assertions: docs/discovery/exploration.md exists with a ## Framings section containing >=3 framings, each carrying an 'origin: user|model' tag
exploration.md has a ## Appetite section (a size, not an estimate) and a ## Decision section (a pick/ranking or an explicit don't-build)
Hard invariant: NO files are written under docs/spec/ (EXPLORE never writes the spine)

evals.name: explore-refusal
evals.prompt: /00-discovery explore — just spec my idea already: an app that reminds freelancers about overdue invoices. Skip the exploration and set up the spine.
evals.expected_output: Despite the pressure to converge immediately, the divergent round still runs: >=3 framings in exploration.md (or an explicit user-override recorded), and STILL no docs/spec/ files. The anti-sycophancy assertion — the skill does not collapse onto idea #1 and start writing a spine on demand.
assertions: The divergent round still ran: exploration.md has >=3 framings despite the converge-now prompt
Hard invariant holds under pressure: NO files under docs/spec/
exploration.md has a ## Appetite and a ## Decision section

evals.name: adopt
evals.prompt: /00-discovery adopt — this repo (notekeep, a small passphrase-locked notes CLI) has no spec. Reverse-engineer a spec spine from the existing code.
evals.expected_output: ADOPT mode: the existing repo is the project, so the spine is written alongside the code. Every REQ is authored from code evidence — an EARS statement line + source: code:<path:line> (or docs:<path>), derived by construction until confirmed — and every code:<path> RESOLVES to a real file (anti-hallucination). The planted dead feature (PDF export in notekeep/export_pdf.py — never wired into the CLI, never documented) is surfaced as an out-of-scope / removal candidate, not silently kept as a REQ. The auth invariant (every note operation requires an unlocked session) is captured as a must-not REQ or a Constitution item. Registry-leaf integrity holds; scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted and exits 0.
assertions: Every REQ statement line is EARS-form and cites adopt evidence (source: code:<path> or docs:<path>), all derived/adopt-confirmed
Anti-hallucination: every adopt code:<path> resolves to a real file in the project
The planted zombie feature (PDF export) is surfaced out-of-scope / for removal, never kept as an active REQ
The auth/session invariant is captured as a must-not (IF/THEN) REQ or a Constitution item
Registry-leaf integrity holds; scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted and exits 0 on the fresh spine

evals.name: agent-brief
evals.prompt: Here's a brief for Relay, a support-triage AI agent (brief.md). Run discovery and produce the project spec spine from it.
evals.expected_output: An agent-system spine: specification.md carries `- **Profile:** agent-system`, and the spine gains docs/spec/agent-contract.md with all six core sections (autonomy tier; risk class; tool-permission matrix with a HITL column and >=1 concrete tool row such as the Stripe refund at HITL: yes; escalation/HITL policy; cost envelope with a token/spend budget + a retry/step cap; memory policy). At least one must-not REQ (IF/THEN Unwanted-behavior) captures a high-risk-tool refusal (no refund without human approval; no ungrounded reply). Latency targets (first-response under a minute) live in architecture-constraints, not the cost envelope (the S8 boundary). Registry-leaf integrity holds; verify-spine emits and exits 0. Baseline produces generic requirements with none of the agent-contract structure.
assertions: specification.md declares `- **Profile:** agent-system`
docs/spec/agent-contract.md exists with all six core section heads (autonomy tier, risk class, tool-permission matrix, escalation/HITL, cost envelope, memory policy)
The tool-permission matrix has a `| Tool | Scopes | Risk | HITL |` header and at least one concrete tool row (e.g. the Stripe refund with HITL: yes)
The cost envelope declares a token/spend budget and a retry/step cap (the runaway-loop defense); latency targets live in architecture-constraints (the S8 boundary)
At least one must-not REQ in the IF/THEN Unwanted-behavior form captures a high-risk-tool refusal (no refund without approval, or no ungrounded reply)
Every REQ statement line is EARS-form; registry-leaf integrity holds; scripts/verify-spine.py is emitted and exits 0 on the fresh spine
